resource.description: Resources are the most important element. Each resource block describes one or more infrastructure objects.

data.description: Data sources allow Terraform to use information defined outside of Terraform or by another configuration.

variable.description: Input variables serve as parameters for a Terraform module. Can have type, default, description, validation.

output.description: Output values are the return values of a Terraform module.

provider.description: Provider configurations specify which provider plugin to use and supply its connection details.

module.description: A module call references a reusable set of Terraform configurations.

locals.description: Local values assign a name to an expression for reuse within a module.

terraform.description: Terraform settings block configures required_version, required_providers, and backend.

for_each.description: Meta-argument that creates an instance for each item in a map or set of strings.

count.description: Meta-argument that creates a specific number of instances of a resource.

depends_on.description: Explicitly specify dependencies between resources that Terraform cannot automatically detect.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle block customizes how resources are created, updated, and destroyed.

provisioner.description: Provisioners execute scripts on local or remote machines as part of resource creation or destruction.

dynamic.description: Dynamic blocks generate repeated nested blocks from a collection.

backend.description: Backends determine where Terraform stores its state. Options: s3, gcs, azurerm, consul, local.

string.description: Strings are sequences of Unicode characters. Support interpolation with ${...} and directive syntax.

number.description: Numbers can be integers or floating-point values.

bool.description: Boolean values representing true or false.

list.description: List/tuple type - an ordered sequence of values.

map.description: Map/object type - a collection of key-value pairs.
